info.name: KSeFFlow
info.description:
KSeFFlow (e-invoicing) API — one folder per controller. Base path /api/v1 on port 8081. Auth is the httpOnly idToken cookie set by the RegulaOne backend: log in via RegulaOne SSO first (Postman cookie jar carries it), or paste a token into {{token}} for the Bearer fallback header.

Also includes the KSeF 2.0 government API (Ministry of Finance) calls the backend makes — base {{ksefGovUrl}} (sandbox https://api-test.ksef.mf.gov.pl/v2). Those use KSeF tokens ({{ksefAuthToken}} / {{ksefAccessToken}} / {{ksefRefreshToken}}), NOT the RegulaOne session — obtain them via the Auth flow (challenge → XAdES → redeem).

Also includes the internal Notification Hub call KSeFFlow makes server-to-server — base {{hubUrl}} (RegulaOne backend, http://localhost:8080). KSeFFlow does NOT store notifications itself; it publishes business events to the Hub, which resolves recipients and delivery. That call is NOT user-authenticated — it is gated by the shared {{serviceToken}} in the X-Service-Token header (must match RegulaOne's NOTIFICATION_INTERNAL_TOKEN).

item.name: Notification Hub (RegulaOne — internal)
item.description: Server-to-server events KSeFFlow publishes to the centralized RegulaOne Notification Hub (POST /api/internal/notifications/events on {{hubUrl}}). NOT user-authenticated — gated by the shared X-Service-Token ({{serviceToken}}). Best-effort: a failed publish never breaks the invoice/retry flow. The Hub resolves WHO receives each event (explicit recipientUserIds, else tenant users holding any audiencePermissions, else the event type's default audience) and applies category/severity defaults for null fields. dedupeKey makes re-publishing idempotent.

body.raw:
{
  "eventType": "INVOICE_SENT_TO_KSEF",
  "tenantId": "TENANT_ID_HERE",
  "sourceModule": "KSEFFLOW",
  "title": "Invoice accepted by KSeF",
  "body": "Invoice FV/2026/05/0001 received KSeF number and UPO.",
  "relatedEntityType": "INVOICE",
  "relatedEntityId": "INVOICE_ID_HERE",
  "dedupeKey": "INVOICE_SENT_TO_KSEF:INVOICE_ID_HERE",
  "occurredAt": "2026-05-01T10:15:30"
}

{
  "eventType": "INVOICE_RETRY_FAILED",
  "tenantId": "TENANT_ID_HERE",
  "sourceModule": "KSEFFLOW",
  "title": "Invoice submission keeps failing",
  "body": "Automatic retries for invoice FV/2026/05/0001 have been exhausted. Manual review needed.",
  "severity": "HIGH",
  "relatedEntityType": "INVOICE",
  "relatedEntityId": "INVOICE_ID_HERE",
  "recipientUserIds": [
    "USER_ID_HERE"
  ],
  "audiencePermissions": [
    "InvoiceWrite"
  ],
  "dedupeKey": "INVOICE_RETRY_FAILED:INVOICE_ID_HERE",
  "occurredAt": "2026-05-01T11:00:00"
}

item.name: Notifications (read — RegulaOne, module=KSEFFLOW)
item.description: How the KSeFFlow UI GETS its notifications. These are the USER-facing endpoints served by RegulaOne (base {{hubUrl}}, http://localhost:8080), NOT KSeFFlow's own backend — KSeFFlow has no notification store. Auth is the RegulaOne idToken cookie (log in via SSO so the cookie jar carries it) or the {{token}} Bearer fallback; every endpoint is forced server-side to the caller's own tenant + user. The 'module' query param is MANDATORY and scopes results to this app — always module=KSEFFLOW here, so KSeFFlow never sees another app's notifications. A missing/unknown module returns 400. (Preferences are account-wide and take no module.)
